data.title: German Industrial Output Rebounds in January as Auto Sector Drives Growth
data.description:
Germany's industrial output rebounded more than expected in January, recovering from the prior month's decline as a surge in automotive production lifted overall activity, provisional data from the Federal Statistical Office showed Monday.

Seasonally and calendar-adjusted industrial production rose 2% on the month, beating analysts' estimates of a 1.5% increase and reversing a revised 1.5% decline in December 2024. On an annual basis, output was still down 1.6% but better than the revised 2.2% drop in the previous month.

The recovery was driven by a 6.4% month-over-month jump in automotive production. The food and machine maintenance and assembly industries also made strong contributions of 7.5% and 15.6%, respectively. However, these gains were partially offset by a 7.7% decline in the production of fabricated metal products.

Despite the rebound in industrial output, Germany's export sector showed signs of weakness. Exports fell 2.5% in January after a revised 2.5% rise in the prior month, leading to a seasonally adjusted trade surplus of 16 billion euros, well below the 21 billion-euro forecast.

ING analysts noted the German industry's recovery but cautioned that near-term risks persist, particularly as the threat of US tariffs on the European Union looms.

"Today's data confirms the bottoming out of Germany's industrial slump. However, it is too early to call any substantial turnaround. Manufacturing capacity utilisation is at lows comparable only to those seen during the financial crisis and the initial lockdowns, order books shrank again in January with particularly weak foreign demand, and inventory levels remain at elevated levels," ING wrote.

"However, if the announced stimulus is indeed delivered, it could provide a short-term confidence boost, and enhance the longer-term prospects of the German economy," analysts added.

data.title: CISI Fin Unit Buys Over $10.1 Million Worth of Bonds
data.description:
China Industrial Securities International Financial Group's unit CISI Investment acquired around $10.1 million of bonds issued by ORIX from the open market for roughly $9.6 million, a Friday bourse filing said.

The bonds, which were purchased between Feb. 12 and March 6 make up part of an issue of $500 million of 4% senior bonds falling due April 13, 2032.

The securities broker bought bonds for investment purposes.
